cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out which of these statements is sarcastic. So, the options are A and B. The statements are about putting people in neat little compartments to protect your viewpoint and whether that's a good idea. First, I need to recall what sarcasm is. Sarcasm is a form of irony where someone says the opposite of what they really mean, often in a mocking or scornful way. It's usually used to express the opposite of one's real thoughts, often with a tone of mockery or contempt. Looking at the first option, (A): "Stop putting people in neat little compartments to protect your view point — good idea". The first part says "Stop putting people in compartments", which is a suggestion
